description_long: This object is a tall coconut palm tree with a slender, dark brown trunk that tapers slightly as it rises. The trunk is topped with a crown of long, arching fronds that radiate outward in all directions, each frond composed of numerous narrow, pointed leaflets. The leaves are a mix of green and yellowish hues, with some subtle brownish tinges, giving a realistic, sun-weathered appearance. The fronds are dense and overlap, creating a lush canopy that partially obscures the top of the trunk. The base of the trunk is slightly flared, suggesting stability and age. No coconuts or visible fruit clusters are present among the fronds, and the tree stands alone without any visible surrounding context.
description: A tall coconut palm tree with a dark, slender trunk and a dense crown of long, green fronds radiating outward. The leaves are detailed and sun-faded, with no visible coconuts or fruit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full spread of the palm fronds, with the trunk centered and the leaves fanning symmetrically.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the right side fronds, with the trunk still mostly visible and the leaf density consistent.
description_view_2: Side view with the fronds appearing more layered and overlapping, giving a sense of depth to the canopy.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the fronds arching outward, showing the back of the canopy and the trunk's vertical alignment.
attribute_husk_pattern: The trunk surface displays a rough, fibrous texture with vertical striations and irregular markings, typical of palm bark. The fronds have a linear, parallel vein pattern with dense, narrow leaflets radiating from a central stem. No distinct coconut husk is visible.
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The object itself is a palm tree; no additional palm trees are visible nearby.
